tone: Bright, mineral, declarative. The breeze in a can — short, warm, sensory. A short sentence with a long after-taste. Brisa never sounds like Volt; we are not loud, slangy, ironic, or aspirational about hustle.
do: Lead with sensory verbs and nouns — peel, stone, morning, lift, crack, open.
Make one claim per surface; leave room for the consumer to bring their own.
Use minerality, not sweetness — stone, citrus peel, morning air over candy or dessert metaphors.
Default to less. If a thing can come out, it comes out.
dont: Don't use wellness jargon or function claims (detox, immune-boosting, fat-burning).
Don't lean on sugar, dessert, or candy-shop language.
Don't borrow Volt's punchy energy-drink tone or hustle posture.
Don't stack adjectives or use category clichés (refreshingly refreshing, burst of flavor).
Don't co-lockup with the Onda parent on consumer surfaces.
promptFragments: soft natural daylight, morning window light
mineral palette — foam, paper, aqua, mineral teal
citrus peel, blood orange zest, yuzu
calm, airy negative space, generous white room
still life, single can, no clutter
